volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Pot of Gold (Section 2.4.307 - Section 2.8.380)

sections.label: Section 2.4.307
sections.translation: Why really , I do believe you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.307b
sections.translation: But , further , do you know how it is ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.308
sections.original: aquam hercle plorat , cum lavat , profundere .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , he grieves to throw away the water when he washes .

sections.label: Section 2.4.309
sections.translation: Do you think a great talent [*]

sections.label: Section 2.4.310
sections.original: ab istóc sene ut det , qui fiamus liberi ?
sections.translation: might be begged of this old fellow for him to give us , through which we might become free ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.311
sections.original: Famem hercle utendam si roges , numquam dabit .
sections.translation: By my troth , if you were to ask it , he would never let you have the loan of hunger . Why , the other day , the barber had cut his nails [*] ; he collected all the parings , and carried them off .

sections.label: Section 2.4.314
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you do describe a miserably stingy wretch .

sections.label: Section 2.4.315
sections.original: Censen vero esse parcum et misere vivere ?
sections.translation: But do you think that he does live so very stingily and wretchedly ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.316
sections.translation: A kite , the other day , carried off his morsel of food ; the fellow went crying to the Praetor [*] ; there , weeping and lamenting , he began to request that he might be allowed to compel the kite to give bail .

sections.label: Section 2.4.320
sections.original: sescenta sunt quae memorem , si sit otium .
sections.translation: There are innumerable other things that I could mention , if I had the leisure . But which of you two is the sharper ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.322
sections.translation: I — as being much the better one .

sections.label: Section 2.4.322b
sections.translation: A cook I ask for , not a thief [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.323
sections.translation: As a cook , I mean .

sections.label: Section 2.4.323b
sections.translation: What do you say ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.323c
sections.translation: I ’ m just as you see me .

sections.label: Section 2.4.324
sections.original: Cocus ille nundinalest , in nonum diem
sections.translation: He ’ s a nine-day cook [*] ; every ninth day

sections.label: Section 2.4.325
sections.translation: he ’ s in the habit of going out to cook .

sections.label: Section 2.4.325b
sections.translation: You , you three-lettered fellow [*] ;

sections.label: Section 2.4.326
sections.translation: do you abuse me , you thief ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.326b
sections.translation: To be sure I do , you trebly-distilled thief of thieves [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.5.327
sections.original: Tace nunciam tu , atque agnum hinc uter est pinguior
sections.translation: Now do you hold your tongue for the present , and , that lamb , whichever is the fatter of the two —

sections.label: Section 2.5.328b
sections.translation: Very well [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.5.328c
sections.translation: Do you , Congrio ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.328a
sections.original: quem illic reliquit agnum , eum sume atque abi
sections.translation: take that , and go in-doors there ( pointing to EUCLIO ’ S house ; and ( to a MUSIC-GIRL and some of the PEOPLE with provisions ) do you follow him ;

sections.label: Section 2.5.330
sections.original: vos ceteri ite huc ad nos .
sections.translation: the rest of you this way , to our house .

sections.label: Section 2.5.330b
sections.translation: By my troth , you ’ ve made an unfair

sections.label: Section 2.5.331
sections.translation: division ; they ’ ve got the fattest lamb .

sections.label: Section 2.5.332
sections.original: At nunc tibi dabitur pinguior tibicina .
sections.translation: But the fattest music-girl shall be given you then . Do you , therefore , go along with him , Phrygia [*] . And do you , Eleusium , step in-doors here , to our house .

sections.label: Section 2.5.334b
sections.translation: O you crafty Strobilus ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.335
sections.original: hucine detrusti me ad senem parcissimum ?
sections.translation: have you pushed me off here upon this most miserly old fellow , where if I ask for anything , I may ask even to hoarseness before anything ’ s found me ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.337b
sections.translation: ’ Tis very foolish , and ’ tis thanklessly done , to do a service to you , when what you do goes for nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.5.339
sections.translation: But how so ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.339b
sections.translation: Do you ask ? In the first place then , there will be no confusion for you there in the house ; if you want anything to use ,

sections.label: Section 2.5.341
sections.original: domo abs te adferto , ne operam perdas poscere .
sections.translation: bring it from your own home , don ’ t lose your trouble in asking for it . But here , at our house , there ’ s great confusion , and a large establishment — furniture , gold , garments , silver vessels . If anything ’ s lost here ( as I know

sections.label: Section 2.5.345
sections.original: facile abstinere posse , si nihil obviam est ),
sections.translation: that you can easily [*] keep hands off — if nothing ’ s in your way ), they may say , The cooks have stolen it ; seize them , bind them , beat them , thrust them in the dungeon [*] . Nothing of that sort will happen to you , inasmuch as there will be nothing for you to steal . Follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 2.5.349b
sections.translation: I follow .

sections.label: Section 2.6.350
sections.original: Heus , Staphyla , prodi atque ostium aperi .
sections.translation: Ho , there Staphyla , come out and open the door .

sections.label: Section 2.6.350b
sections.translation: Who calls there ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.351
sections.translation: Strobilus .

sections.label: Section 2.6.351b
sections.translation: What do you want ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.351c
sections.translation: For you to take in these cooks , and this music-girl , and these provisions for the wedding . Megadorus bade me take these things to Euclio .

sections.label: Section 2.6.354
sections.original: Cererin , Pythodice , has sunt facturi nuptias ?
sections.translation: Are you about to make this wedding , Strobilus , in honor of Ceres [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.355
sections.translation: Why ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.355b
sections.translation: Because I don ’ t see any wine brought .

sections.label: Section 2.6.356
sections.original: At iam afferetur , si a foro ipsus redierit .
sections.translation: Why , that will be brought just now , when he himself comes back from the market .

sections.label: Section 2.6.357
sections.original: Ligna hic apud nos nulla sunt .
sections.translation: There ’ s no firewood here in our house .

sections.label: Section 2.6.357b
sections.translation: There are the beams .

sections.label: Section 2.6.358
sections.translation: I ’ faith , there are .

sections.label: Section 2.6.358b
sections.original: Sunt igitur ligna , ne quaeras foris .
sections.translation: There is wood , then ; don ’ t you be seeking it out of doors .

sections.label: Section 2.6.359
sections.translation: What , you unpurified fellow [*] , although your business is with the fire ,

sections.label: Section 2.6.360
sections.original: cenaene causa aut tuae mercedis gratia
sections.translation: for the sake of a dinner , or of your own hire , do you request us to set our house on fire ?

sections.label: Section 2.6.362
sections.translation: I don ’ t ask you .

sections.label: Section 2.6.362b
sections.translation: Take them in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.6.362c
sections.translation: Follow me .

sections.label: Section 2.7.363
sections.original: Curate . ego intervisam quid faciant coqui ;
sections.translation: Mind you your business ; I ’ ll step in and see what the cooks are doing , to observe whom , i ’ faith , to-day it is a most laborious task .

sections.label: Section 2.7.365
sections.original: nisi unum hoc faciam , ut in puteo cenam coquant :
sections.translation: Unless I manage this one thing , for them to cook the dinner down in the dungeon [*] ; thence , when cooked , we might bring it up again in small baskets . But if they should eat below [*] whatever they should cook , those above would go without their dinner , and those below have dined . But here am I chattering , as though I had no business ,

sections.label: Section 2.7.370
sections.original: rapacidarum ubi tantum sit in aedibus .
sections.translation: when there ’ s such a pack of thieves in the house .

sections.label: Section 2.8.371
sections.original: Volui animum tandem confirmare hodie meum ,
sections.translation: I wished at length to screw up my courage to-day , so as to enjoy myself at the wedding of my daughter . I come to the market , I enquire about fish ; they tell me that it is dear , that lamb is dear , beef is dear ,

sections.label: Section 2.8.375
sections.translation: veal , large fish [*] , and pork , all of them are dear . And for this reason were they still dearer ; I hadn ’ t the money . I came away thence in a rage , since I had nothing wherewithal to make a purchase ; and thus did I baulk [*] all those rascals . Then I began to think with myself upon the road ,

sections.label: Section 2.8.380
sections.original: occepi : festo die si quid prodegeris ,
sections.translation: If you are guilty of any extravagance on a festive day , you may be wanting on a common day , unless you are saving . After I disclosed this reasoning to my heart and appetite , my mind came over to my opinion , that I ought to give my daughter in marriage at as little expense as possible .
